name: Las Vegas: The Center of the Neon Strip

stops.name: Fountains of Bellagio
stops.shortDescription: A spectacular choreographed water feature set in an 8-acre artificial lake. The highest-shooting nozzles can blast water up to 460 feet into the Las Vegas sky.

stops.name: Caesars Palace
stops.shortDescription: An iconic Roman-themed luxury hotel and casino opened in 1966. Its Colosseum theater was custom-built for Celine Dion and features one of the largest LED screens in North America.

stops.name: The LINQ Promenade
stops.shortDescription: A bustling open-air retail and entertainment district. It is anchored by the High Roller, a 550-foot-tall observation wheel that takes 30 minutes to complete a single revolution.

stops.name: Flamingo Wildlife Habitat
stops.shortDescription: A serene, 15-acre tropical oasis hidden in the middle of a casino. It houses a flock of live Chilean flamingos, brown pelicans, and colorful koi fish.

stops.name: Eiffel Tower Viewing Deck
stops.shortDescription: A half-scale replica of the famous Parisian landmark standing 46 stories tall. It offers stunning, unobstructed 360-degree views of the Strip and the surrounding Nevada desert.

stops.name: The Cosmopolitan
stops.shortDescription: A sleek, modern resort known for its chic design and multi-story crystal chandelier bar. It famously features a secret, unnamed pizzeria hidden down an unmarked hallway on the third floor.

stops.name: ARIA Resort & Casino
stops.shortDescription: The centerpiece of the CityCenter complex, featuring stunning contemporary architecture, a massive fine art collection, and advanced smart-room technology.

stops.name: The Shops at Crystals
stops.shortDescription: An upscale shopping mall boasting a striking jagged glass facade. It houses exclusive high-end retailers and impressive indoor water and ice sculptures.

stops.name: MGM Grand Bronze Lion
stops.shortDescription: The largest bronze statue in the United States, standing 45 feet tall. It guards the entrance to what is currently the single largest hotel building in the world.

stops.name: New York-New York
stops.shortDescription: Features a magnificent skyline replica of 1940s Manhattan, including the Statue of Liberty and the Empire State Building, with a thrilling roller coaster weaving through it.

stops.name: Excalibur Hotel & Casino
stops.shortDescription: A massive, whimsical castle-themed resort inspired by the legend of King Arthur, complete with brightly colored turrets and a medieval jousting dinner show.

stops.name: Luxor Hotel & Casino
stops.shortDescription: An iconic 30-story black glass pyramid accompanied by a giant replica of the Great Sphinx. From its peak, it projects the strongest beam of light in the world.

stops.name: Welcome to Fabulous Las Vegas Sign
stops.shortDescription: The world-famous neon sign designed by Betty Willis in 1959. Located at the southern end of the Strip, it is an absolute must-visit photo opportunity to conclude the tour.
